title: DSDS Variants Document Block
description: Every way a component can be configured — a toggle (flag variant, like 'disabled') or a set of options (enum variant, like size: sm | md | lg). Each can explain why it exists.

variantValue.description: A single option within an enum variant dimension.

description.description: What this value looks like, when to use it, and how it differs from other values in this dimension.

rationale.description: Why this value exists (e.g., 'High emphasis directs users to the one key action'). For guidance comparing values, use `use-cases` instead.

examples.description: Examples showing this variant value in isolation or in context.

tokens.description: Token overrides applied when this value is selected.

flagVariant.description: A toggle — on or off. Use it for binary capabilities like 'disabled' or 'full-width'. No `values` array; it's simply active or not.

kind.description: Identifies this variant as a boolean flag.

name.description: Human-readable name of the flag (e.g., 'Disabled', 'Full Width', 'Icon Only', 'Loading').

description.description: What this flag controls and how it affects the component's appearance or behavior when active.

rationale.description: Why this flag exists (e.g., 'Prevents interaction during submission to avoid duplicate requests').

examples.description: Examples showing the component with this flag active.

tokens.description: Token overrides applied when this flag is active.

enumVariant.description: A set of options where you pick one, like 'size' (sm | md | lg) or 'emphasis' (primary | secondary | ghost).

kind.description: Identifies this variant as an enumerated set of values.

identifier.description: Machine-readable name for this dimension (e.g., 'size', 'emphasis') — the axis, not one of its values.

name.description: Human-readable name of the variant dimension (e.g., 'Size', 'Emphasis', 'Shape').

description.description: What this dimension of variation controls and how its values affect the component's appearance or behavior.

values.description: The possible values. A single value is fine — dimensions often start with just one while more are added over time. Tools SHOULD keep this order.

variants.description: Every way a component or pattern can be configured — a toggle or a set of options. Multiple items combine independently. A variant is a choice the consumer makes up front (e.g., 'size', 'full-width'); a condition entered at runtime (hover, disabled, loading) is a state — document that in `states` instead.

kind.description: Identifies this block as a variants spec.

items.description: The variant dimensions, in order. Tools SHOULD keep this order.
One variant dimension. `kind: 'flag'` makes it a toggle; `kind: 'enum'` makes it a set of options. We use `if`/`then` instead of `oneOf` so an invalid entry gives one clear error instead of many.
